question_text: Which statement correctly compares gas pressure in two identical containers at different temperatures?

options.label: A
options.text: The container at 20°C has higher pressure because it's cooler and particles are more tightly packed

options.label: B
options.text: The container at 40°C has higher pressure because particles move faster and collide with walls more forcefully

options.label: C
options.text: Both containers have equal pressure because they're the same size

options.label: D
options.text: The container at 20°C has higher pressure because cooler particles move downwards

correct_answer: The container at 40°C has higher pressure because particles move faster and collide with walls more forcefully
explanation: When gas temperature increases, particles gain more kinetic energy and move faster. Faster-moving particles collide with container walls more frequently and with greater force, creating higher pressure. This is a key relationship in gas behaviour: pressure increases with temperature in a fixed volume.
